revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Okinawa_Gokoku_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社| 名称=沖縄県護國神社| 所在地=沖縄県那覇市奥武山町44| 祭神=郷土関係靖國の御英霊
沖縄方面作戦にて殉じた本土出身の御英霊
戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=昭和11年（1936年）| 画像=[[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_haiden.jpeg|250px|沖縄県護國神社拝殿]]}} '''沖縄県護國神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。沖縄県関係の護国の[[英霊]]だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っており、2005年10月現在の祭神の数は17万8689柱である。 ==歴史== 昭和11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた英霊を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護國神社に改称した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）4月に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が行われた。 [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|left|200px|境内]] [[Category:沖縄県の神社|おきなわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|おきなわけんここくしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=沖縄県護國神社| 所在地=沖縄県那覇市奥武山町44| 祭神=郷土関係靖國の御英霊

{{神社| 名称=沖縄県護国神社| 所在地=沖縄県那覇市奥武山町44| 祭神=郷土関係靖國の御英霊
沖縄方面作戦にて殉じた本土出身の御英霊
戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=昭和11年（1936年）| 画像=[[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_haiden.jpeg|250px|沖縄県護國神社拝殿]]}} '''沖縄県護国神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。沖縄県関係の護国の[[英霊]]だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っており、2005年10月現在の祭神の数は17万8689柱である。 [[神社本庁]]などの包括宗教法人に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 ==歴史== 昭和11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた英霊を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護国神社に改称した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）4月に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が行われた。 [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|left|200px|境内]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ 沖縄県護国神社] {{Commonscat|Okinawaken Gokoku-jinja|沖縄県護国神社}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おきなわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:沖縄県の旧県社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:那覇市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 沖縄県護国神社 |画像 = [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_haiden.jpeg|250px|沖縄県護國神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 沖縄県那覇市奥武山町44 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|26|12|11.3|N|127|40|33.9|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 郷土関係靖國の御英霊

{{神社 |名称 = 沖縄県護国神社 |画像 = [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_haiden.jpeg|250px|沖縄県護國神社拝殿]]

戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = 昭和11年（1936年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月23日・10月23日 |神事 = }} '''沖縄県護国神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[神社本庁]]などの包括宗教法人に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 沖縄県関係の護国の[[英霊]]だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っており、[[平成]]17年（[[2005年]]）10月現在の祭神の数は17万8689柱である。 == 歴史 == [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|left|170px|境内]] [[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた英霊を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護国神社に改称した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）4月に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が行われた加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Okinawaken Gokoku-jinja|沖縄県護国神社}} *[http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ 沖縄県護国神社]（神社公式） {{DEFAULTSORT:おきなわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:沖縄県の旧県社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:那覇市]] {{shinto-stub}}

戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = 昭和11年（1936年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月23日・10月23日 |神事 = }} '''沖縄県護国神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[神社本庁]]などの包括宗教法人に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 沖縄県関係の護国の[[英霊]]だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っており、[[平成]]17年（[[2005年]]）10月現在の祭神の数は17万8689柱である。 == 歴史 == [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|RIGHT|170px|境内]] [[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた英霊を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護国神社に改称した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）4月に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が行われた加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Okinawaken Gokoku-jinja|沖縄県護国神社}} *[http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ 沖縄県護国神社]（神社公式） {{DEFAULTSORT:おきなわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:那覇市]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:沖縄県の旧県社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:琉球ゴールデンキングス]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/松島芭蕉|松島芭蕉]] ([[User talk:松島芭蕉|会話]]) による ID:47480235 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]] カテゴリ設置要件（分類or関連の深さ）に不十分。宣伝行為。

戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = 昭和11年（1936年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月23日・10月23日 |神事 = }} '''沖縄県護国神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[神社本庁]]などの包括宗教法人に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 沖縄県関係の護国の[[英霊]]だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っており、[[平成]]17年（[[2005年]]）10月現在の祭神の数は17万8689柱である。 == 歴史 == [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|RIGHT|170px|境内]] [[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた英霊を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護国神社に改称した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）4月に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が行われた加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Okinawaken-Gokoku-jinja|沖縄県護国神社}} *[http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ 沖縄県護国神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おきなわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:那覇市]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:沖縄県の旧県社]] [[Category:単立神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 沖縄県護国神社 |画像 = [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_haiden.jpeg|250px|沖縄県護國神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 沖縄県那覇市奥武山町44 | 緯度度 = 26 | 緯度分 = 12 | 緯度秒 = 11.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 127 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 33.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Naha city |祭神 = 郷土関係靖國の英霊
沖縄方面作戦にて殉じた本土出身の英霊
戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = 昭和11年（1936年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月23日・10月23日 |神事 = }} '''沖縄県護国神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[神社本庁]]などの包括宗教法人に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 沖縄県関係の戦死者だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っている。[[平成]]17年（[[2005年]]）10月現在の祭神の柱数は17万8689柱で、護国神社では最多である。 == 歴史 == [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|境内]] [[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた死者を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護国神社に改称した『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）[[4月26日]]に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日、11月20日に復興奉祝祭が行われた。 昭和55年（[[1980年]]）[[9月21日]]には、神楽殿が竣工した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} *[http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ 沖縄県護国神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おきなわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:沖縄県の旧県社]] [[Category:単立神社]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gogoku Shrine haiden.jpeg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead revision.comment: Replacing Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_haiden.jpeg with [[File:Okinawaken_Gokoku_Shrine_haiden.jpg]] (by [[commons:User:CommonsDelinker|CommonsDelinker]] because: [[:c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[:c:COM:FR#FR3|Criterion 3]] (obvious error)).

「Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_haiden.jpeg」 を 「Okinawaken_Gokoku_Shrine_haiden.jpg」 に差し替え（[[commons:User:CommonsDelinker|CommonsDelinker]]による。理由：[[:c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[:c:COM:FR#FR3|Criterion 3]] (obvious error)）
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 沖縄県護国神社 |画像 = [[image:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|沖縄県護國神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 沖縄県那覇市奥武山町44 | 緯度度 = 26 | 緯度分 = 12 | 緯度秒 = 11.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 127 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 33.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Naha city |祭神 = 郷土関係靖國の英霊
沖縄方面作戦にて殉じた本土出身の英霊
戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = 昭和11年（1936年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月23日・10月23日 |神事 = }} '''沖縄県護国神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[神社本庁]]などの包括宗教法人に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 沖縄県関係の戦死者だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っている。[[平成]]17年（[[2005年]]）10月現在の祭神の柱数は17万8689柱で、護国神社では最多である。 == 歴史 == [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|境内]] [[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた死者を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護国神社に改称した『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）[[4月26日]]に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日、11月20日に復興奉祝祭が行われた。 昭和55年（[[1980年]]）[[9月21日]]には、神楽殿が竣工した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} *[http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ 沖縄県護国神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おきなわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:沖縄県の旧県社]] [[Category:単立神社]]

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox shrine | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | address = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deities = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.comment: Turning most of the links here into interwiki links, because we are currently on the shinto wiki and these off topic links are causing issues with redlink tracking. change back if imported to wikipedi

Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | address = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deities = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox shrine | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | address = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox shrine | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox shrine | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox shrine | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] }} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:new They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds.{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Former prefectural
