{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市|12=draft}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|12=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] [[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}

As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|12=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市|12=draft}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|12=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Standalone Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] [[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|12=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Standalone Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] [[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}

The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市|12=draft}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|12=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Standalone Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] [[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft|12=draft}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft|12=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft|12=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{ill|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]] [[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]]

A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{ill|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku revision.comment: 32 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:沖縄県護国神社 up to rev 98802554
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 沖縄県護国神社 |画像 = [[image:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|沖縄県護國神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 沖縄県那覇市奥武山町44 | 緯度度 = 26 | 緯度分 = 12 | 緯度秒 = 11.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 127 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 33.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Naha city |祭神 = 郷土関係靖國の英霊
沖縄方面作戦にて殉じた本土出身の英霊
戦争の犠牲となった一般住民・遭難学童および文官関係戦歿者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = 昭和11年（1936年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月23日・10月23日 |神事 = }} '''沖縄県護国神社'''（おきなわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]]の[[奥武山公園]]内にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[神社本庁]]などの包括宗教法人に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 沖縄県関係の戦死者だけでなく、[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[沖縄戦]]に殉じた本土出身者や犠牲となった一般市民も祭神として祀っている。[[平成]]17年（[[2005年]]）10月現在の祭神の柱数は17万8689柱で、護国神社では最多である。 == 歴史 == [[image:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|境内]] [[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]以降の国難に殉じた死者を祀るために創建された[[招魂社]]に始まる加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日 。昭和15年（[[1940年]]）7月1日の内務省令により内務大臣指定護国神社となり沖縄県護国神社に改称した『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997年3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）4月の沖縄戦により社殿を焼失し、昭和34年（[[1959年]]）[[4月26日]]に仮社殿を竣工した。同年秋の大祭にて、[[靖国神社]]より沖縄戦に殉じた本土出身者の御霊代を奉移した。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）10月、現在の社殿が竣工し、11月19日に遷座祭が加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000年10月30日、11月20日に復興奉祝祭が行われた。 昭和55年（[[1980年]]）[[9月21日]]には、神楽殿が竣工した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} *[http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ 沖縄県護国神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おきなわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:那覇市の建築物]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:沖縄県の旧県社]] [[Category:単立神社]]

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft|12=draft}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{ill|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄県護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft|12=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|12=draft|12=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|沖縄県護国神社|version=98802554|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{ill|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ revision.comment: marking informing about the history on everybodywiki
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = 1936 | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Okinawaken Gokoku Shrine haiden.jpg|250px|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden | coordinates = {{Coord|26|12|11.6|N|127|40|33.6|E|scale:25000_region:JP-47_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 奥武山町44, Naha City, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = * Local war dead enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * War dead from mainland Japan who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Civilian victims and student casualties of the war | established = 1936 (Shōwa 11) | festivals = April 23, October 23 }} '''Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|沖縄県護国神社|1=Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|Okinawaken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located within {{ill|Onoyama Park|ja|奥武山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Naha|ja|那覇市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is an independent religious institution not affiliated with [[Jinja Honcho]]. {{ill|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} The Okinawa Gokoku-jinja Shrine also enshrines the dead of the [[Battle of Okinawa]], including ordinary residents, schoolchildren in distress, and civilian war dead.[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、212頁{{Cite web |date=2022-03-08 |title=沖縄県護国神社について｜初詣、ご祈願、地鎮祭は、沖縄県護国神社 |url=https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308190702/https://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/about.php |archive-date=8 March 2022 |access-date=2022-03-08 |website=}} In addition, about 10,000 mobilized students and female volunteer corps members who were victims of the [[Atomic bomb]] are also enshrined as deities at [[Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]].[[護国神社#山中|山中 2013]]、154頁{{Cite web |date=2021-01-20 |title=由緒 {{!}} 広島護國神社 |url=https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120233005/https://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/yuisho/ |archive-date=2021-01-20 |access-date=2022-03-08}} The main hall burned down during the [[Pacific War]] and was reconstructed in 1959.{{Cite book |last=Takenaka |first=Akiko |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824856939 |title=Yasukuni Shrine |date=2017-10-16 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-5693-9 |pages=158}} == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines spirits of war dead from Okinawa Prefecture and mainland Japan, including civilians and student casualties who perished during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of October 2005, the shrine enshrines 178,689 spirits, the highest number among {{ill|Gokoku shrines|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} nationwide. == History == [[File:Okinawaken_Gogoku_Shrine_torii.jpeg|thumb|right|170px|Torii gate]] The shrine traces its origins to a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established in 1936 to honor those who died in service to Japan during conflicts such as the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p115　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1940, it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Ministry of Home Affairs and renamed "Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine."『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）287頁。 The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|ja|沖縄戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in April 1945. A temporary shrine was constructed on April 26, 1959. In the same year, relics of spirits from the {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were transferred to the shrine, honoring mainland Japanese who died in the Battle of Okinawa. The current main shrine building was completed in October 1965, with the relocation ceremony held on November 19 and the restoration commemorative festival on November 20.加治順人『沖縄の神社』p143　ひるぎ社、2000th year 10月30日 In 1980, a Kaguraden (dance hall) was added to the grounds. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.okinawa-gokoku.jp/ Official website of Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} }} {{Okinawan Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Okinawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|沖縄県護国神社|version=98802554|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Naha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Independent Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Naha]] [[Category:History of Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:1875 establishments]] [[Category:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Standalone Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Okinawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:沖縄県護国神社]]
